title: Geriatric Polypharmacy Case

patient.name: Ny. KW
patient.comorbidities: HT, T2DM, CKD stage 3a, right knee OA, BPH-like LUTS

severitySummary: SEVERE — 3 hard alerts, 2 high alerts requiring immediate deprescribing review

streamBlocks.text: Clinical Decision Support — Drug Interaction & Deprescribing Analysis

72-year-old female with CKD stage 3a (eGFR 48), orthostatic hypotension (systolic drop 26 mmHg on standing), and 9 concurrent medications from 3 prescribers. Primary concern today: orthostatic dizziness × 2 weeks.

streamBlocks.title: DRP-1: Glibenclamide + eGFR 48 + age 72
streamBlocks.text: Long-acting sulfonylurea in elderly with reduced renal function — high risk of severe, prolonged hypoglycaemia.
streamBlocks.mechanism: Glibenclamide has active metabolites renally excreted; impaired clearance at eGFR <60 prolongs hypoglycaemic effect.
streamBlocks.recommendation: STOP Glibenclamide. Replace with Linagliptin 5 mg OD (no renal dose adjustment required).
streamBlocks.reference: Beers Criteria 2023 — avoid long-acting sulfonylureas in adults ≥65.

streamBlocks.title: DRP-4: Triple Whammy — NSAID + ACE-I + Diuretic on CKD
streamBlocks.text: Meloxicam (NSAID) + Captopril (ACE-I) + Furosemide on eGFR 48 — severe AKI risk.
streamBlocks.mechanism: ACE-I reduces efferent arteriolar tone; diuretic causes volume depletion; NSAID blocks prostaglandin-mediated afferent vasodilation — combined effect sharply reduces GFR.
streamBlocks.recommendation: STOP Meloxicam. Replace with Paracetamol 500 mg PRN (max 3 g/day) + topical Diclofenac gel for knee.
streamBlocks.reference: Triple whammy AKI — well-documented in CKD populations (eGFR <60).

streamBlocks.title: DRP-5: Tamsulosin in female patient — off-label
streamBlocks.text: α1-blocker prescribed for LUTS in female — likely mis-prescribed; major contributor to orthostatic hypotension.
streamBlocks.mechanism: α1-adrenergic blockade causes vasodilation and postural BP drop, compounded by antihypertensives and diuretic.
streamBlocks.recommendation: STOP Tamsulosin pending urology verification. Observe orthostatic symptoms for 1 week.
streamBlocks.reference: Tamsulosin is indicated for BPH in males; off-label use in females requires specialist confirmation.

streamBlocks.title: DRP-2: Furosemide without active indication
streamBlocks.text: Routine loop diuretic without current edema — contributes to volume depletion and orthostatic hypotension.
streamBlocks.mechanism: Volume depletion reduces preload; combined with antihypertensives and α-blocker worsens postural BP drop.
streamBlocks.recommendation: STOP or reduce to PRN only if edema recurs.
streamBlocks.reference: Orthostatic hypotension polypharmacy: ≥2 antihypertensives + α1-blocker + diuretic.

streamBlocks.title: DRP-6: Aspirin without primary prevention indication
streamBlocks.text: Self-purchased aspirin in 72-year-old with no CV event history — bleeding risk outweighs benefit.
streamBlocks.mechanism: Primary prevention aspirin in elderly increases GI bleeding risk without proven CV benefit in this profile.
streamBlocks.recommendation: STOP aspirin. Educate patient on lack of indication.
streamBlocks.reference: ASPREE trial and current guidelines — avoid primary prevention aspirin in adults >70 without high CV risk.

streamBlocks.title: DRP-3: Captopril TID dosing in elderly
streamBlocks.text: Short-acting ACE-I with suboptimal adherence in elderly — consider once-daily alternative.
streamBlocks.mechanism: TID dosing reduces compliance; trough BP control may be inadequate.
streamBlocks.recommendation: Switch Captopril 25 mg TID → Lisinopril 10 mg OD.
streamBlocks.reference: Fornas preference for once-daily antihypertensives in chronic disease management.

streamBlocks.text: Recommended Regimen After Deprescribing (9 → 4 core drugs)

items: Amlodipine 5 mg — 1× daily AM (reduced from 10 mg)
Paracetamol 500 mg PRN — max 3 g/day (replaces Meloxicam)
Topical Diclofenac gel — PRN for knee

references.title: Beers Criteria 2023 — American Geriatrics Society
references.detail: Avoid long-acting sulfonylureas (Glibenclamide) in adults ≥65 with CKD.

references.title: Triple Whammy AKI — Nephrology literature review
references.detail: ACE-I/ARB + diuretic + NSAID combination in CKD stage 3+ — incidence and prevention.

references.title: Fornas 2024 — Formularium Nasional
references.detail: Linagliptin, Lisinopril dosing guidance for Indonesian BPJS facilities.
